actionable_feedback: Explicitly state and justify the chosen normalization denominator (currently 'embedded maximum'); either (a) reference an authoritative packet field that mandates this denominator, (b) present an explicit rationale for the max-to-100 choice, or (c) include an alternative normalization option and show sensitivity to that choice in the memo and workbook.

Add an explicit cap and tie-breaker rules for remediation-improvement multipliers applied to copy_ratio (e.g., do not allow >100% improvement; document rounding/ceil rules and order of applying combos).

Declare an absolute droplet QC threshold (or justify why relative droplet normalization is sufficient) and document how droplet QC is applied to calls. If thresholds are advisory, mark them as such and flag rows that would change under a realistic absolute QC.

Provide formula-level traceability for critical calculations: include either (a) an exported plain-text listing of the key formulas used for scoring/classification/remediation in assay_call.md, or (b) a 'Traceability' sheet in the workbook that maps each output cell to its source embedded-packet field and shows the formula text. This helps reviewers when openpyxl is unavailable.

Include a small reproducible CSV or JSON data export and a short calculation script or pseudocode (embedded into assay_call.md) so reviewers can recompute scores without spreadsheet parsing libraries.
Confirm and document rounding rules used for reported scores and combo max_score values (precision and rounding direction).
artifact_specific_comments: assay_call.md: strong content and explicit needs_review flags; add explicit formula text or formula mapping to improve reproducibility.
ddpcr_copy_number.xlsx: workbook has expected sheets per xlsx_validation, but environment lacked openpyxl - include a plain-text formula export and mapping sheet to mitigate parser unavailability.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are present and well-structured (assay_call.md plus workbook). Graders and verifier concur that the packet documents scoring, classification thresholds, sensitivity checks, remediation combos, and explicitly flags ambiguities. Minor, actionable issues remain: (1) normalization denominator was assumed (embedded maximum) and needs justification or alternatives; (2) remediation copy_ratio improvement multiplier lacks an explicit cap and tie-breaker rules; (3) absolute droplet QC threshold is not specified; and (4) spreadsheet formula-level traceability is limited in environments where openpyxl is unavailable. Addressing these will improve reproducibility and reviewer confidence.

recommended_artifact_additions: Traceability sheet mapping calculated cells to source fields and formulas
CSV/JSON reproduction of input rows and a calculation script/pseudocode
extra_model_fields.verifier_notes_summary: No substantive grounding issues; grader claims are supported by artifact previews and actual_outputs. Limitations due to workbook parsing are documented and mitigatable by adding text-based formula exports.
